name: Retune The Accepted Grouped-Row=8 PTX Hot Band Around Stage Cadence

summary.name: Retune The Accepted Grouped-Row=8 PTX Hot Band Around Stage Cadence
summary.hypothesis: Round 47's grouped-row=8 PTX hot-band path remains the accepted best base at 25.529328 ms, and round 48 showed that keeping one more mirrored-column B fragment live is not the right next lever: mio_throttle only moved 4.77 -> 4.72 and lts throughput only moved 30.02 -> 30.11 while runtime regressed to 25.759745 ms. With DRAM still low at 10.35%, tensor activity only 48.21%, warps active only 16.64%, and barrier still 7.52%, the next highest-upside move is to keep the accepted grouped-row=8 traversal and shift to a structural stage/cadence experiment in the dominant 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel. The existing two-K staged 128x128 path is the most relevant template: borrow its stage turnover or wait/commit cadence, but do not reintroduce B-fragment lookahead as the main mechanism.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization and steady-state K16 stage turnover in the dominant grouped-row=8 PTX hot-band kernel, which is still underfeeding Tensor Cores after the locality win.
summary.risk: This is the highest-upside but also the most structural option. A cadence change can raise shared-memory footprint, lengthen live ranges, perturb occupancy, or undo the current grouped-row=8 stability. The implementation must stay anchored to the accepted grouped-row=8 PTX path rather than reopening the failed B-fragment-live branch.
metrics_to_recheck: median runtime and TFLOP/s against the 25.529328 ms accepted base and the <20 ms user target
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the cadence change lowers barrier a little but does not beat 25.529328 ms, or if it improves one stall counter by trading it for higher shared/register pressure.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
